name: search_docs
description: Full-text search across the knowledge base. Returns the documents matching the query, ranked by relevance. Use terms in the language of the indexed content.

tag.description: Optional: only keep results that also carry this tag (folder-derived or frontmatter, case-insensitive).

name: read_doc
description: Read the full raw content of a document (.md OR .html). For a .html you get the complete HTML source (not extracted text).

path.description: Relative path of the .md or .html (e.g. notes/example.md, projects/deck-may.html). Use list_tree or search_docs to discover paths.

name: list_tree
description: List the full tree of the knowledge base (metadata only, no content). Handy to understand the folder organization before navigating.

name: recent_docs
description: Recently modified documents, newest first. Handy to know what the user has worked on lately.

name: create_doc
description:
Create a new document. Two formats, pick by content:
• .md — prose, notes, recaps, specs, markdown reports (rendered with marked in the viewer). Default.
• .html — a SELF-CONTAINED styled document: slide deck, dashboard, richly laid-out page with its own CSS/JS. Rendered AS-IS in a viewer iframe sandbox.
RULE: NEVER wrap a full HTML document (<!DOCTYPE html>…) inside a ```html code block of a .md — it would show as raw source. Create a .html instead.
Refuses if the path already exists (create-only, no overwrite). The path must end in .md or .html and be in a valid folder (notes/, projects/, inbox/, etc.).

path.description: Relative path ending in .md or .html (e.g. inbox/note.md, projects/deck-report.html)

content.description: For a .md: full markdown body with an H1 title. For a .html: a complete self-contained HTML page (<!DOCTYPE html>…</html>), inline CSS/JS included.

name: edit_doc
description:
Edit an EXISTING document (.md OR .html). Two mutually exclusive modes:
1. Targeted replacement: pass 'old_string' + 'new_string'. 'old_string' must appear exactly once in the document (otherwise an error) — add surrounding context to make it unique. Token-efficient, preferred on large docs (and .html, often big).
2. Full rewrite: pass 'content' (replaces the whole file).
At least one of the two modes is required. The document must already exist (use create_doc to create a new one). Read the doc with read_doc before editing.

path.description: Relative path of the existing .md or .html (e.g. notes/recap.md, projects/deck-may.html)

old_string.description: Patch mode: the exact text to replace, must be unique in the document.

new_string.description: Patch mode: the replacement text (required if old_string is provided).

content.description: Rewrite mode: the full new markdown content. Ignored if old_string is provided.

name: move_doc
description: Move OR rename an existing document (.md or .html), AUTOMATICALLY rewriting the incoming [[wikilinks]] that point at it (no broken backlinks). Renaming = a move within the same folder (just change the filename in 'to'). Refuses if the target already exists (no overwrite). Read list_tree/search_docs first for the exact path.

from.description: CURRENT relative path of the .md (e.g. notes/draft.md)

to.description: NEW relative .md path (e.g. projects/draft-final.md). Same folder + new name = a plain rename.

name: get_links
description: Outgoing [[wikilinks]] of a document: the docs it points to. Lets you traverse the mind's graph forward from a doc.

name: get_backlinks
description: Backlinks of a document: the docs that point TO it via [[wikilinks]]. Find what references a doc.

name: get_mind_topology
description: Bird's-eye view of the whole mind's graph (a summary, not the full graph): doc/edge counts, density, hubs (most-referenced docs), orphans (no link), and the most frequent tags. Call it on first contact with a mind to get oriented before diving in.

name: list_by_tag
description: List the documents carrying a given tag (folder-derived or frontmatter). Navigate the mind by topic, like a human browsing folders.

name: delete_doc
description: Delete a document — SOFT delete: it is moved to a .trash/ folder (reversible), never erased. To rename, use move_doc instead. Refuses on remote mirrors (remotes/). Called by an AI, so the move-to-trash keeps a wrong call recoverable.

path.description: Relative path of the .md/.html to delete
